liveDiagnostic.assistantTurnInterpretation: Cabinet persisted its bounded failure turn; no ACP assistant chunk was observed.

exactExit124Producer.mapping: src/lib/agents/adapters/hermes-runtime.ts maps timeout classification to exitCode 124

firstDivergence.standalone: The authorized live probe gave initialization the configured 120000 ms budget and completed startup in about 549.9 ms.
firstDivergence.integrated: The production copy reused the generic Hermes 3000 ms source timeout for initialization. The diagnostic observed initialize dispatch but no initialize response before the adapter stopped the child.
firstDivergence.consequence: Session creation and model prompt dispatch never happened, so provider/model startup was not the measured failure.

minimumProductionChange.summary: Extract one shared official-SDK ACP transport core and have both the standalone probe and Cabinet adapter call it. Give ACP initialization its explicit passing-path deadline instead of reusing the 3000 ms read-only Hermes source timeout.
minimumProductionChange.promptTimeoutPolicy: Keep the conversation turn deadline separate; do not increase it and do not add a retry.
scope: shared child launch and environment builder
minimumProductionChange.whyMinimum: The live diagnostic failed before session creation. No prompt, persistence, route, or model behavior needs to change to clear this measured gate.

sharedCoreFinding.finding: The integration branch contains a copied approximation of the passing probe, not a shared transport module.
evidence: The passing implementation lives only on research/persistent-acp-sdk-transport under experiments/transports/acp-sdk/src/probe.ts.
The integration implementation is a separate src/lib/hermes/acp-client.ts with separately maintained launch, timeouts, frame guard, lifecycle, and notification normalization.
The two implementations import the same official SDK version but have no shared source import.

executable.note: The exact local absolute path was verified identical and is redacted from the artifact by the private-path reporting rule.

executable.standalone: CPython 3.12.11 arm64 from the companion venv
executable.integrated: CPython 3.12.11 arm64 from the same companion venv

processLaunch.behavioralImpact: Not reached beyond initialize; cwd is sent again at session creation.
Both contain the same minimal model/provider configuration shape.

processLaunch.integrated: derived by the identical companion wrapper from isolated HOME

processLaunch.standalone: base process fields plus the one provider credential and explicit isolated Hermes fields
processLaunch.integrated: base process fields plus the one provider credential; wrapper freezes Hermes fields
processLaunch.behavioralImpact: Effective provider credential, profile, no-tools, HOME, and Hermes home were present in both.

processLaunch.standalone: three pipes, NDJSON over stdin/stdout, stderr drained
processLaunch.integrated: three pipes, NDJSON over stdin/stdout, stderr drained

processLaunch.standalone: Node Web Streams plus SDK NDJSON stream and 1 MiB frame guard
processLaunch.integrated: Node Web Streams plus SDK NDJSON stream and 1 MiB frame guard

processLaunch.behavioralImpact: No independent detached ACP process in either path.

processLaunch.integrated: SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 2000 ms, plus 15-minute idle stop

acpProtocol.integrated: rejected during initialization when forbidden fs/terminal capability names appear

acpProtocol.standalone: session/new with cwd and empty mcpServers
acpProtocol.integrated: session/new with cwd and empty mcpServers
load when a persisted native session is not already loaded in the server-owned client

acpProtocol.standalone: session/prompt with one text content block
acpProtocol.integrated: session/prompt with one text content block

acpProtocol.standalone: session/update text chunks, tool fail-closed, dedupe metrics
acpProtocol.integrated: session/update text chunks to Cabinet transcript, tool fail-closed, silent dedupe

acpProtocol.standalone: prompt result plus 25 ms notification drain and non-empty output assertion
acpProtocol.integrated: prompt result plus 25 ms notification drain and non-empty output assertion

acpProtocol.standalone: session/cancel on prompt deadline with metric
acpProtocol.integrated: session/cancel on prompt deadline without metric
error stop, explicit pool shutdown, or idle stop

timeouts.note: The POST returns after scheduling; it did not produce 124.
Not reached as the failing deadline.

timeouts.standaloneMs: SDK request without a separate wrapper deadline
timeouts.integratedMs: SDK request without a separate wrapper deadline

timeouts.note: No model request occurred in the diagnostic.
The earlier authoritative harness observed the adapter's 124 before its own poll deadline.

persistence.integrated: Cabinet-generated conversation id persisted in isolated data
ACP session id returned as adapter session id and persisted as resumeId

persistence.standalone: one probe instance owns one active connection
persistence.integrated: module map from native session id to persistent client
prepareSession loads a supplied id only when not already loaded
new Cabinet process receives persisted resumeId, starts a client, then session/load
persistence.note: Not exercised because initialization failed on the first turn.
